evals.prompt: Add a user story: as a requestor I want to cancel a submitted request before it has been approved, because I sometimes realize I ordered the wrong samples after submitting. Acceptance criteria: cancel button only shown for status=Submitted requests; cancellation sets status to Cancelled; cancelled requests cannot be re-edited.

assertions.text: A new file docs/notes/us###.md was created in the sandbox
Filename slug matches us<NNN>.md with three-digit zero-padded sequential id
ID does not collide with existing us001/us002/us003/us013/us014 (so id is us015 or higher)
YAML frontmatter contains aliases (list), status, created keys
status frontmatter value is 'draft'
created frontmatter value matches ISO date YYYY-MM-DD
H1 includes [[product]] wikilink
## role section contains wikilink [[pn001|requestor]] referencing the existing persona
## want section describes cancelling a submitted request
## because section explains realizing wrong samples / ordered incorrectly
## acceptance_criteria has exactly 3 bullets matching the user's input
Footer contains 'Index: [[product]]'

evals.prompt: Capture this as a story — the warehouse picker wants to mark a request as picked once items are pulled from inventory, because that signals it's ready for handoff.

assertions.text: A new file docs/notes/us###.md was created
Filename slug is us<NNN>.md with three-digit zero-padded id
Frontmatter aliases entry is a human-readable title describing the pick action
status frontmatter is 'draft'
## role wikilink either references an existing persona alias (e.g. requestor, approver) OR a newly-created pn### that matches 'picker' or 'warehouse'
## want describes marking the request as picked
## because gives a non-empty motivation (mentions handoff or ready-for-pickup)
## acceptance_criteria has at least 1 bullet (derived if user did not specify, with disclosure)
Footer contains 'Index: [[product]]'

evals.prompt: Add my first story: as a guest I want to view the public catalog without signing in, because I want to evaluate the products before creating an account.

assertions.text: A new file docs/notes/us001.md was created (first story of empty workspace)
Filename is exactly us001.md, not us<larger>.md
Frontmatter contains aliases (with at least one entry), status: draft, created: ISO date
H1 ends with [[product]] wikilink
## role section references a persona via wikilink (existing or newly created pn###)
## want describes browsing the public catalog without auth
## because mentions evaluation / pre-account browsing
## acceptance_criteria has at least 1 testable bullet
Footer contains 'Index: [[product]]'

evals.prompt: Add a story. Context: we're on Next.js 14 / Postgres 16 / Drizzle ORM. The requestor wants to export their request history as a CSV — they keep getting asked for expense reports by accounting and screenshotting the dashboard is tedious. The CSV should have one row per request and include the date, status, and total line-item count. Probably a GET /api/requests/export endpoint, served from the same auth-protected route as the dashboard. Use pn001.

assertions.text: A new file us###.md was created in the seeded sandbox under docs/notes/
## role section references [[pn001|requestor]]
## want section describes the outcome (export request history / get a CSV / etc.) and does NOT mention Next.js, Drizzle, Postgres, /api/, or 'endpoint'
## because section captures the motivation (expense reports / accounting asks / screenshotting is tedious)
## acceptance_criteria has at least 2 bullets

No AC bullet mentions: 'Next.js', 'Drizzle', 'Postgres', '/api/', 'GET ', 'POST ', 'endpoint', 'route', '.tsx', '.ts', 'middleware', 'foreign key', 'table', 'column', 'index' — i.e. AC stays in problem-space, not solution-space
No AC bullet starts with imperative-on-developer verbs ('Add ', 'Create ', 'Implement ', 'Build ', 'Use ', 'Wire ')
AC bullets describe observable behavior (the requestor can do X, the CSV contains Y, the export fails when Z) — subject is the user or the system as observed by the user, not the developer
Confirmation flags that AC were derived (user didn't supply them) — does NOT silently invent without disclosure
Footer contains 'Index: [[product]]'

evals.prompt: Add a user story: as a requestor I want to attach a PDF receipt to a request so the approver has documentation. Acceptance criteria: 1) Add a 'Attach receipt' button to the request form; 2) POST /api/requests/{id}/attachments accepts multipart/form-data and returns 201; 3) Store the file in S3 under requests/{id}/{uuid}.pdf; 4) Add an attachments column to the requests table linking to attachment ids. Use pn001.

assertions.text: A new file us###.md was created
## role section references [[pn001|requestor]]
## want describes attaching a receipt (preserves the user's outcome-shaped want)
## because mentions documentation / the approver having context
## acceptance_criteria contains the user's wording — user-supplied bullets are NOT silently rewritten into problem-space

The skill's response (response.md or equivalent) explicitly flags that the AC are solution-shaped — calls out at least one of the user's bullets as reading like a solution / implementation detail and offers to rephrase, instead of just writing the file and moving on

The flag text mentions at least one of these phrases: 'solution', 'implementation', 'rephrase', 'spec-writing decides HOW', 'observable behavior' — i.e. the user is told this is a problem-vs-solution issue, not just a 'looks weird' complaint
If the skill offered to rephrase, the original user wording is preserved in the on-disk file (the user must consent before the file is rewritten)
Footer contains 'Index: [[product]]'

evals.prompt: Capture this as a story: as a requestor I want a 'Cancel' button on the request detail view, because I sometimes need to cancel after submitting. Use pn001.

assertions.text: A new file us###.md was created
## role references [[pn001|requestor]]
## want is preserved verbatim OR the user was offered an alternative phrasing (e.g. 'cancel a submitted request before approval') and they chose

The skill's response (response.md or equivalent) flags the want as solution-shaped ('the want describes a UI element, not an outcome') AND suggests an outcome-shaped alternative — does NOT silently rewrite, does NOT just accept without comment

The flagged phrasing distinguishes between 'what the user wants to achieve' (outcome) and 'how the UI shows it' (Cancel button) — i.e. shows the model understands the distinction, not just keyword-matched on 'button'
Footer contains 'Index: [[product]]'

evals.prompt: Add a user story: as a requestor I want to clone a previous request as a starting point for a new one, because most of my orders look almost identical to past ones. Acceptance criteria: clone button visible on closed requests; clone copies line items but leaves notes blank; cloned request starts in Draft status.

assertions.text: A new file us###.md was created under <sandbox>/main/docs/notes/ (the main worktree's AKM dir)
NO new us*.md was created under <sandbox>/feat/docs/notes/ (the feature worktree's tree is untouched)
Filename slug matches us<NNN>.md with three-digit zero-padded id and is us015 or higher (does not collide with seeded ids us001-us014)
The new file is staged in the main worktree's git index (git -C <sandbox>/main status --porcelain shows the file as 'A ' or 'AM ')
No commit was created on main for this story (git -C <sandbox>/main log --oneline shows only the seed commit, not a new story commit) — story-write only stages, the next lifecycle stage commits
## role section contains [[pn001|requestor]] referencing the existing seeded persona
## acceptance_criteria has exactly 3 bullets matching the user's input
Footer contains 'Index: [[product]]'
Skill confirmation surfaces the absolute AKM path so the user sees the write landed in <sandbox>/main, not <sandbox>/feat
